{{Infobox character
|nom=Footman
|apparition=Appears in book 1
|status=Unknown
}}

== Biography ==

The Footman is a servant stationed outside the Duchess's residence. He engages with visitors in absurdly logical conversation, arguing that knocking on a door has no sense when the door lies between the knocker and the person inside—observing that if one were already inside, they might knock to be let out. When a plate comes flying out of the house and grazes his nose, breaking against a nearby tree, he continues speaking in exactly the same tone as if the incident had not occurred, displaying remarkable imperturbability amid chaos.

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Personnages du Tome 1]]
